events.label: reasoning
events.text:
We need answer JSON only. Need decide fail because DP impulse lines sloped wrong for service: gas taps below pipe / liquid taps on top. This is classic non-compliant for differential pressure flow measurement. API RP 551 and manufacturer manuals require impulse lines slope to avoid gas pockets in liquid service and liquid traps in gas/vapor service. For gas service, taps should be on top of pipe, lines slope back to pipe or have condensate pots. For liquid service, taps on side/bottom, lines slope up to transmitter or have gas vents. Here condition is reversed: gas taps below pipe (will collect liquid) and liquid taps on top (will trap gas). So non-compliant, installed-defective. Work stage rough-complete. Risk? Could cause measurement error, process control issues, maybe high if safety critical. But typical manufacturing floor, maybe medium/high. Need choose. Since wrong slope for service can cause inaccurate readings and potential process upset, risk medium or high. Let's assess: DP transmitters used for flow/level. Gas service with taps below pipe => condensate accumulation, false DP, possible liquid slug. Liquid service with taps on top => gas pocket, erratic readings. In manufacturing/assembly floor standard work quality gates, non-compliant. Risk maybe high due to measurement integrity and process control. But not immediate physical danger. The schema includes high. Let's pick high. Confidence 0.95. Percent complete maybe 70 (roughed in). Component conditions: installed-defective, non-compliant. Findings: Impulse tubing routed with incorrect slope/orientation for service; gas service impulse taps located below pipe centerline allowing condensate collection; liquid service taps located at top of pipe allowing gas pocketing. Actions: Re-route/re-orient impulse lines per API RP 551 and manufacturer requirements; for gas service locate taps at top and slope lines back to process or provide condensate pots/drains; for liquid service locate taps at side/bottom and slope lines to high-point vents or transmitter; re-inspect before close-out. References: API RP 551, manufacturer installation manuals. Need include all fields. value 0, sound_source "", event_time 0, rate 0, order [], workable [].

Need ensure JSON valid. No prose outside.

  "findings": [
    "DP impulse lines are sloped/oriented incorrectly for the intended service.",
    "Gas service impulse taps are located below the pipe, creating a liquid trap that will foul the DP measurement.",
    "Liquid service impulse taps are located on top of the pipe, creating a gas pocket that will cause erratic or false readings."
  ],
  "actions": [
    "Re-route and re-orient the impulse lines per API RP 551 and the transmitter manufacturer installation manual.",
    "For gas/vapor service, locate taps at the top of the pipe and slope lines back to the process or provide condensate pots/drains.",
    "For liquid service, locate taps at the side or bottom of the pipe and slope lines to vent gas at high points or at the transmitter manifold.",
    "Re-inspect the installation before closing out the work package."
  ],
  "rationale": "Impulse tubing must be arranged so gas lines stay free of condensate and liquid lines stay free of gas. The observed configuration is the reverse of what API RP 551 and manufacturer guidance require, so the installation is defective and non-compliant.",
  "references": [
    "API RP 551",
    "Manufacturer transmitter installation manuals"
  ]
}
